extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA Filippo won the 11.5km flat ITT by 28 seconds over KELDERMAN, with late starters benefiting from weather improvement and drier roads.
tactical_patterns: Late starters gained significant advantage as rain stopped and roads dried, creating 40-second gaps among GC contenders.
INEOS placed two riders on podium (Ganna, Sheffield) showing depth in short flat ITT specialists.
McNulty positioned second American behind Sheffield at turn, suggesting UAE pacing strategy prioritized first half conservatively.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: 28-second margin suggests holding blue jersey into mountain stages feasible for large TT specialist
rider_signals.SHEFFIELD Magnus: positive: set early benchmark holding against accomplished riders until late weather-aided runs
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: positive: 40-second gap over teammate Yates signals GC hierarchy shift within UAE
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: conceding 40 seconds to McNulty on flat ITT reveals vulnerability in non-climbing disciplines
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: second place confirms time trial form retained despite GC ambitions
rider_signals.HEPBURN Michael: positive: split two Americans showing Jayco TT depth continues 2023 success pattern
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: third place confirms reinvention as TT specialist beyond mountain climbing youth pedigree
